core_takeaway: Agent_1's first-speaker baseline hardened into the deal: Jewel+Quill stayed with Agent_1, Agent_2 got its desired Pencil+Apple, and Agent_3 accepted only Stone because future rebalancing was promised and the competing proposals left it with nothing.
why_or_counterexample: Yes. The process shows more than a numeric win: Agent_1 anchored on keeping Jewel+Quill, later speakers treated that as a constraint, every formal proposal preserved it, and private votes accepted Agent_1's proposal as the only one giving Agent_3 any immediate value. The advantage is partly positional and partly preference-compatible because Agent_2 also benefited from the same baseline.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened by naming Jewel+Quill as its top pair and proposing a baseline that kept both. Agent_2 quickly aligned because it received Pencil+Apple. Agent_3 initially wanted Apple+Pencil but by its final discussion turn accepted the baseline if a Round-2 path existed.
phase_notes.private_thinking: All agents' private reasoning converged on Option A as most likely to reach unanimity. Agent_3 privately recognized that this left it far from Apple+Pencil but still planned to push the baseline with future rebalancing.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Agent_1 proposed the eventual allocation. Agent_2 and Agent_3 submitted a different allocation, but even those preserved Agent_1's top pair and disadvantaged Agent_3 more severely.
phase_notes.private_voting: Private votes unanimously accepted Proposal 1 and rejected Proposals 2 and 3. The system tabulation counts show Proposal #1 at 3 accept / 0 reject, but the consensus sentence incorrectly says Proposal #3; final_allocation and raw votes match Proposal #1.
phase_notes.reflection: No reflection section is present; assigned metadata reports reflection_entries=0.

mechanisms.theme: First-speaker anchor made Jewel+Quill the default
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 used the first speaking turn to define the agenda around keeping its top pair, then reinforced that boundary before proposals. That anchor survived the rest of the process.

evidence.quote: Here are my thoughts as the first speaker.
I take Jewel and Quill (my top two).
I will not give up Jewel or Quill in Round 1.
It preserves my top two items

mechanisms.theme: Later speakers treated Agent_1's protected pair as a constraint
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_2 and Agent_3 did not seriously dislodge Jewel+Quill after Agent_1's opening. They negotiated mainly over Apple/Pencil/Stone while leaving Agent_1's allocation intact.

evidence.quote: This keeps your top two with you
keeping Jewel + Quill with you.
I can work with that framing
I would accept the baseline Option A

mechanisms.theme: Future-round promises deferred Agent_3's high-value claims
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_3's Apple+Pencil demand was acknowledged but pushed into a possible Round 2. That let Agent_1 keep its present high-value allocation while Agent_3 accepted a very low current payoff.

evidence.quote: Apple and Pencil are my top priorities
give Agent_3 Apple + Pencil
short, explicit Round-2 plan
Round 2 revisits a potential rearrangement

mechanisms.theme: Formal proposal set protected Agent_1 across alternatives
mechanisms.explanation: The formal proposal space was already narrowed: every submitted proposal gave Agent_1 Jewel+Quill. The non-Agent_1 proposals made Agent_3 even worse off, strengthening the acceptability of Agent_1's version.

evidence.quote: allocation: {'Agent_1': [1, 3], 'Agent_2': [4, 0], 'Agent_3': [2]}
Preserves Agent_1's top pair Jewel(1) and Quill(3)
preserves Agent_1's top two items
Agent_3: (no items)

mechanisms.theme: Voting made Agent_1's proposal the only viable consensus
mechanisms.explanation: The raw votes accepted Agent_1's proposal because it satisfied Agent_2 and at least gave Agent_3 a positive foothold. Alternatives were rejected because they left Agent_3 with zero immediate value.

evidence.quote: Proposal #1: 3 accept, 0 reject
Gives me Pencil + Apple (my top two items)
the only positive allocation among the three
Proposal 2 assigns Agent_3 nothing

mechanisms.explanation: This is not a pure order effect. Agent_2 independently liked the same baseline because it maximized Agent_2's own Round-1 utility, so Agent_1's advantage was amplified by compatible incentives.

evidence.quote: maximizes my own utility to 63
the best possible result for me
give me Pencil+Apple (my two strongest items)
